sections.heading: Generate an Autocompletion Script
sections.body:
**Bash:**

Bash

To learn how to generate the autocompletion script for Bash,
see [atlas completion bash](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-completion-bash).

**Fish:**

fish

To learn how to generate the autocompletion script for fish,
see [atlas completion fish](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-completion-fish).

**Powershell:**

PowerShell

To learn how to generate the autocompletion script for PowerShell,
see [atlas completion powershell](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-completion-powershell).

**Zsh:**

zsh

To learn how to generate the autocompletion script for zsh,
see [atlas completion zsh](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-completion-zsh).

sections.heading: Create and Manage Atlas CLI Profiles
sections.body:
Use the following resources to create and manage profiles.

For step-by-step instructions on saving connection settings as
profiles, see [Save Connection Settings](atlas/cli/stable/atlas-cli-save-connection-settings).

sections.heading: Log In to the Atlas CLI
sections.body:
To authenticate with Atlas using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas auth login [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas auth login](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-auth-login).

sections.heading: Update a Profile in the Terminal
sections.body:
To update values in an Atlas CLI profile using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas config set <propertyName> <value> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas config set](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-config-set).

sections.heading: Update a Profile in a Text Editor
sections.body:
To open the config file for your Atlas CLI profile with the default text editor using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas config edit [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas config edit](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-config-edit).

sections.heading: View a Profile
sections.body:
To return the details of the Atlas CLI profile you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas config describe <name> [options]

```

To list all Atlas CLI profiles using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas config list [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas config describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-config-describe) and [atlas config list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-config-list).

sections.heading: Rename a Profile
sections.body:
To rename an Atlas CLI profile using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas config rename <oldProfileName> <newProfileName> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas config rename](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-config-rename).

sections.heading: Log Out of the Atlas CLI
sections.body:
To log out of your Atlas account using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas auth logout [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas auth logout](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-auth-logout).
